terms.definition: A weighted mean of domain-specific integrity scores representing the overall alignment of a civilization’s critical subsystems.
Any semi-autonomous social domain (e.g., family, trust network) whose internal dynamics contribute to systemic coherence.
The minimum composite coherence value below which systemic fragmentation accelerates nonlinearly.
A rapid, nonlinear shift in system order parameters triggered when control variables cross threshold values.
The practice of labeling claims with their evidentiary status (EMPIRICAL, STRUCTURAL, PROVISIONAL, BOUNDARY).
The individual coherence value assigned to a specific subsystem before aggregation.
